data.title: REG - Imperial Brands PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 5038H Imperial Brands PLC 13 November 2025  

IMPERIAL BRANDS PLC

Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) No. 549300DFVPOB67JL3A42

Transaction in own shares

Imperial Brands PLC (the "Company") announces that on 13 November 2025 it purchased for cancellation the following number of its ordinary shares of 10 pence each pursuant to its GBP 1.45 billion share repurchase programme, details of which were announced on 30 October 2025.

Description of shares: Imperial Brands PLC - ordinary shares of 10 pence

Number of shares repurchased: 151,748

Date of transaction: 13 November 2025

Average price paid per share: GBp 3,204.59

Lowest price paid per share: GBp 3,181.00

Highest price paid per share: GBp 3,218.00

Broker: Morgan Stanley & Co. International Plc ("Morgan Stanley")

The Company intends to cancel these Shares.

All shares were purchased from Morgan Stanley as an on exchange transaction subject to the rules of the London Stock Exchange.

Following the purchase and subsequent settlement and cancellation of these shares, the remaining number of ordinary shares in issue will be 802,310,707 (excluding treasury shares).

The figure of 802,310,707 may be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), detailed information about the individual purchases is attached to this announcement.

http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/5038H_1-2025-11-13.pdf

John Crosse     +44 (0) 7484967842

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise as Production Hits Record
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

U.S. crude oil inventories increased more than expected last week as U.S. production hit a record high and exports fell, according to data released Thursday by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Commercial crude oil stocks excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 6.4 million barrels, to 427.6 million barrels, in the week ended Nov. 7, and were about 4% below the five-year average for the time of year, the EIA said. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey had expected a 400,000 barrel crude stock build.

U.S. crude oil production rose by 211,000 barrels a day, to just under 13.9 million barrels a day, the highest on record, according to EIA estimates. Crude imports were down by 703,000 barrels a day, at 5.2 million barrels a day, while exports fell by 1.6 million barrels a day, to 2.8 million barrels a day.

Oil held in the SPR increased by 798,000 barrels, to 410.4 million barrels. Stocks at Cushing, Okla., the Nymex delivery hub, were down by 346,000 barrels, at 22.5 million barrels.

Refinery capacity use rose by 3.4 percentage points, to 89.4%, against expectations in the Journal survey for a 0.6 perentage-point increase. Crude input to refineries was up by 717,000 barrels a day, at 16 million barrels a day.

Gasoline inventories fell by 945,000 barrels, to 205.1 million barrels, and were 4% below the five-year average, the EIA said. Gasoline demand averaged 9 million barrels a day, up by 145,000 barrels a day from the week before. Gasoline stocks were seen falling by 2 million barrels.

Distillate fuel stocks fell by 637,000 barrels, to 110.9 million barrels, and were 8% below the five-year average. Distillate inventories were forecast to have declined by 1.5 million barrels.

 
Change in U.S. oil inventories for the week ended Nov. 7: 
 
                   Crude       Gasoline      Distillates        Refinery Use 
EIA data:           6.4          -0.9           -0.6                 3.4 
Forecast:           0.4          -2.0           -1.5                 0.6

Note: Numbers in millions of barrels, with the exception of refinery use, which is in percentage points.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: Recover Investment Losses: Class Action Initiated Against Cytokinetics, Incorporated (CYTK)
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 13, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Cytokinetics, Incorporated investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/cytokinetics-incorporated-lawsuit-submission-form-2?prid=177236&wire=1&utm_campaign=24

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Cytokinetics, Incorporated that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between December 27, 2023 and May 6, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, defendants made materially false and misleading statements regarding the timeline for the New Drug Application ("NDA") submission and approval process for aficamten. Specifically, defendants represented that the Company expected approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ("FDA") for its NDA for aficamten in the second half of 2025, based on a September 26, 2025 PDUFA date, and failed to disclose material risks related to the Company's failure to submit a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy ("REMS") that could delay the regulatory process.

On May 6, 2025, during an earnings call, it was revealed that the Company had multiple pre-NDA meetings with the FDA discussing safety monitoring and risk mitigation but chose to submit the NDA without a REMS, relying on labeling and voluntary education materials. This confirmed defendants' awareness of potential REMS requirements and their reckless decision to omit it from the initial submission, misleading investors about the regulatory timeline.

As a result of defendants' false and misleading statements, class members purchased Cytokinetics' common stock at artificially inflated prices and suffered significant losses when the truth was revealed.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Cytokinetics stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/cytokinetics-incorporated-lawsuit-submission-form-2?prid=177236&wire=1&utm_campaign=24 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

data.title: Fitch Ratings: Elevated Vehicle Recalls a Near-Term Headwind for Car Rental Companies
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York/Toronto-13 November 2025: Elevated recalls of U.S. rental fleet vehicles are creating near-term earnings and operational headwinds for car rental operators such as The Hertz Corporation (B-/Negative) and Avis Budget Group, Inc. (Not Rated), according to Fitch Ratings. Sustained recall trends could undermine fleet ongoing modernization strategies to reduce depreciation per unit (DPU) and improve customer satisfaction, potentially delaying EBITDA margin improvement for both operators.

The automotive industry experienced approximately 9.8 million vehicle recalls in 3Q25, the highest level since early 2024, per the U.S. Department of Transportation, with rental operators disproportionately affected due to their concentration in recently manufactured vehicles. Ford dominated recall activity, accounting for roughly 55% of affected vehicles in 3Q25. The increase in recalls is being driven by increased vehicle complexity, software integration issues, and supply chain interdependencies across original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).

<br/><br/><br/>Recalled vehicles create extended out-of-service periods that exceed typical maintenance downtime, directly pressuring fleet utilization and fleet management strategies. The timing uncertainty around parts availability and repair completion prevents accurate near-term capacity planning, compounding operational complexity. Car rental companies have limited recourse to recover recall-related losses from OEMs. While OEMs would provide repairs of recalled vehicles, there is no mechanism for OEMs to compensate rental operators for lost utilization, revenue, or operational costs during extended out-of-service periods.<br/><br/><br/>While Hertz and Avis maintain diversified OEM relationships that partially mitigate recall exposure, operational severity differed in 3Q25 and is expected to persist into 2026. Avis reported 5% of its Americas rental fleet under recall, more than Hertz’s 2% rate, with roughly two-thirds of Avis’s recalled vehicles awaiting parts, explicitly reducing utilization and pressuring fleet costs. Avis’ greater exposure suggests concentration in OEM models experiencing more severe parts shortage or recalls in higher-revenue vehicle segments. While Hertz achieved strong utilization of 84% in 3Q25, helped by fleet tightening and process improvement, the firm expects recalls to persist in the near term, weighing on vehicle utilization.<br/><br/><br/>Due to recall uncertainty, government shutdown impacts and company-specific developments (e.g., Hertz’s system outages), both firms have lowered their guidance. However, recall-driven supply constraints could potentially help pricing and partially offset revenue pressures through improved industry rental rate discipline.<br/><br/><br/><br/>Fitch expects EBITDA margins to improve to the single-digit range in 2026, consistent with pre-pandemic levels, assuming recall rates abate and OEM parts supply chains stabilize in the near term. We expect both operators to continue to focus on disciplined fleet management, revenue optimization, and cost control.<br/><br/>While elevated recalls are credit negative for near-term operating performance and margin improvement, the impact will be mitigated by adequate liquidity. As of 3Q25, Hertz maintained $2.2 billion in corporate liquidity while Avis held $1 billion, both supplemented by vehicle borrowing capacity. Near-term liquidity needs remain modest as both operators have largely completed fleet rotation, with moderate ongoing fleet capex needs. Avis has no corporate debt refinancing needs until July 2027, while Hertz faces $500 million in senior unsecured notes maturing December 2026, although this was partially addressed through the issuance of $375 million in exchangeable notes in September 2025. Vehicle-backed ABS trusts of both remain well-cushioned above the disposition and market value test thresholds.<br/><br/>Still, the sector remains constrained by inherent earnings volatility and cyclicality tied to economic conditions and travel demand fluctuations. Residual value risk and depreciation exposure from vehicle supply-demand dynamics are also ratings factors, as is reliance on secured, wholesale funding sources that can become disrupted during periods of market stress, with high funding costs driven by elevated interest rates. Execution risk in fleet management, particularly around DPU optimization and fleet rotation timing, are additional watch items.<br/><br/>Contacts:<br/>Henry Ye, CFA, CAIA<br/>Director, Non-Bank Financial Institutions<br/>+1 647-800-9167<br/><a href="https://mailto:henry.ye@fitchratings.com" target="_blank">henry.ye@fitchratings.com</a><br/>Fitch Ratings, Inc., Canada Subsidiary: Fitch Ratings Canada, Inc.<br/>22 Adelaide St W, Suite 2810, ON M5H 4E3<br/><br/>Laura Kaster, CFA<br/>Senior Director, Risk<br/>North and South American Financial Institutions<br/>Credit Commentary & Research<br/>+1 646 582 4497<br/><a href="https://mailto:laura.kaster@fitchratings.com" target="_blank">laura.kaster@fitchratings.com</a><a href="https://www.fitchratings.com/" target="_blank"/><br/><br/><br/><br/><br/><br/>Media Relations: Cristina Bermudez, New York, Tel: +1 212 612 7892, Email: cristina.bermudez@thefitchgroup.com<br/><br/>The above article originally appeared as a post on the Fitch Wire credit market commentary page. The original article can be accessed at www.fitchratings.com. All opinions expressed are those of Fitch Ratings.<br/><br/><br/>All Fitch Ratings (Fitch) credit ratings are subject to certain limitations and disclaimers. Please read these limitations and disclaimers by following this link: https://www.fitchratings.com/understandingcreditratings. In addition, the following https://www.fitchratings.com/rating-definitions-document details Fitch's rating definitions for each rating scale and rating categories, including definitions relating to default. Published ratings, criteria, and methodologies are available from this site at all times. Fitch's code of conduct, confidentiality, conflicts of interest, affiliate firewall, compliance, and other relevant policies and procedures are also available from the Code of Conduct section of this site. Directors and shareholders’ relevant interests are available at https://www.fitchratings.com/site/regulatory. Fitch may have provided another permissible or ancillary service to the rated entity or its related third parties. Details of permissible or ancillary service(s) for which the lead analyst is based in an ESMA- or FCA-registered Fitch Ratings company (or branch of such a company) can be found on the entity summary page for this issuer on the Fitch Ratings website.<br/><br/>In issuing and maintaining its ratings and in making other reports (including forecast information), Fitch relies on factual information it receives from issuers and underwriters and from other sources Fitch believes to be credible. Fitch conducts a reasonable investigation of the factual information relied upon by it in accordance with its ratings methodology, and obtains reasonable verification of that information from independent sources, to the extent such sources are available for a given security or in a given jurisdiction. The manner of Fitch's factual investigation and the scope of the third-party verification it obtains will vary depending on the nature of the rated security and its issuer, the requirements and practices in the jurisdiction in which the rated security is offered and sold and/or the issuer is located, the availability and nature of relevant public information, access to the management of the issuer and its advisers, the availability of pre-existing third-party verifications such as audit reports, agreed-upon procedures letters, appraisals, actuarial reports, engineering reports, legal opinions and other reports provided by third parties, the availability of independent and competent third- party verification sources with respect to the particular security or in the particular jurisdiction of the issuer, and a variety of other factors. Users of Fitch's ratings and reports should understand that neither an enhanced factual investigation nor any third-party verification can ensure that all of the information Fitch relies on in connection with a rating or a report will be accurate and complete. Ultimately, the issuer and its advisers are responsible for the accuracy of the information they provide to Fitch and to the market in offering documents and other reports. In issuing its ratings and its reports, Fitch must rely on the work of experts, including independent auditors with respect to financial statements and attorneys with respect to legal and tax matters. Further, ratings and forecasts of financial and other information are inherently forward-looking and embody assumptions and predictions about future events that by their nature cannot be verified as facts. As a result, despite any verification of current facts, ratings and forecasts can be affected by future events or conditions that were not anticipated at the time a rating or forecast was issued or affirmed. Therefore, ratings and reports are the collective work product of Fitch and no individual, or group of individuals, is solely responsible for a rating or a report. The rating does not address the risk of loss due to risks other than credit risk, unless such risk is specifically mentioned. Fitch is not engaged in the offer or sale of any security. All Fitch reports have shared authorship. Individuals identified in a Fitch report were involved in, but are not solely responsible for, the opinions stated therein. The individuals are named for contact purposes only. A report providing a Fitch rating is neither a prospectus nor a substitute for the information assembled, verified and presented to investors by the issuer and its agents in connection with the sale of the securities. Ratings may be changed or withdrawn at any time for any reason in the sole discretion of Fitch. Fitch does not provide investment advice of any sort. Ratings are not a recommendation to buy, sell, or hold any security. Ratings do not comment on the adequacy of market price, the suitability of any security for a particular investor, or the tax-exempt nature or taxability of payments made in respect to any security. Such fees generally vary from US$1,000 to US$750,000 (or the applicable currency equivalent) per issue. data.title: High economic growth key for Poland amid fiscal slippage, S&P says
data.description:
By Karol Badohal

Maintaining strong economic growth is crucial for Poland, as its fiscal tightening prospects look slim due to political constraints, S&P Global ratings agency told Reuters.

Last Friday, S&P affirmed Poland's outlook at "stable", citing a balance between strong medium-term growth prospects over the next two years and rising debt risks.

The decision surprised many analysts after Fitch and Moody's in September revised Poland's outlook to "negative", citing a wide fiscal budget deficit and mounting debt.

"We now have a weaker fiscal assessment, both on fiscal performance and on debt. But we also improved the economic assessment," Ludwig Heinz, S&P Global Ratings sovereign analyst, said in an interview this week.

RISING DEBT

Still, he called the increase in Poland's debt "very rapid". S&P expects general government debt to rise to 72.3% of gross domestic product in 2028 from 55.1% last year.

"For us, if (the debt increase) is not accompanied by the projected very high GDP growth, then, of course, I think that the downside pressures could rise," he said.

Poland has one of Europe's fastest growing economies. S&P expects it to expand by 3.3% this year, slowing to 2.8% by 2028.

"So far domestic demand has been one of the key growth drivers this year, while there's a mixed picture certainly in the export-oriented sector," he said, adding S&P would eye exports over the next quarters, also given many external headwinds in other European economies.

Poland's gross domestic product grew 3.7% year-on-year in the third quarter, the first estimate from the statistics office showed on Thursday, up from a 3.3% rise in the second quarter.

Poland's economy benefits from a large domestic market, allowing it to partially decouple from the stagnating German economy, he said.

"With the large EU fund inflows, particularly next year, when the bulk of the RRF (EU Resilience and Recovery Facility) is coming and then the boost to investment, it looks at the moment unlikely to us that there's a macroeconomic adjustment on the horizon for Poland."

Poland's political landscape makes fiscal consolidation unlikely in the next two years, Heinz said.

"The run-up to the 2027 (parliamentary) elections seems to have started," he said, making it difficult for the government to raise taxes or cut spending.

A standoff between Poland's government and its new president has added to the risks, he said.

data.title: Enel Lifts Guidance After International Operations Boost Earnings
data.description:
By Mauro Orru

Enel raised its annual guidance for a key profitability metric after posting higher earnings for the first nine months, aided by its operations in Spain and Colombia.

The Rome-based energy company said net ordinary income this year should come in slightly above the high end of a range between 6.7 billion and 6.9 billion euros ($7.77 billion-$8 billion).

Meanwhile, ordinary earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--another closely watched metric of operating profitability--are still expected to range between 22.9 billion and 23.1 billion euros.

The upgrade comes after Enel said net ordinary income for the first nine months grew 4.5% on year to 5.70 billion euros. Reported net profit came in at 5.24 billion euros compared with 5.25 billion euros a year earlier.

Ebitda fell slightly to 16.87 billion euros from 16.92 billion euros, but ordinary Ebitda rose 0.9% to 17.26 billion euros. Enel said its operations in Spain and Colombia had more-than offset a reduction of margins in Italy.

Revenue increased to 59.70 billion euros from 57.63 billion euros a year earlier, mainly thanks to higher commodity sales on the wholesale market amid rising average prices.

Enel said it approved an interim dividend for 2025 of 0.23 euros a share to be paid starting from Jan. 21.

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com

data.title: Alternative Measure of October Inflation Finds It 'Remains Firm but Not Frightening' — WSJ
data.description:
By Harriet Torry

There's no consumer-price index out today-as originally scheduled before the government shutdown-but one of the private alternatives that scrapes millions of prices from across the web suggests inflation remained relatively benign in October.

Called PriceStats, the price index-which is managed by State Street Global Markets and generally tracks the CPI- increased 0.16% last month, similar to its 0.24% increase in September. The PriceStats number isn't seasonally adjusted.

"For now, inflation remains firm but not frightening," said Michael Metcalfe, head of macro strategy at State Street.

On an unadjusted basis, the government's official consumer-price index rose 0.3% in September from the prior month, the last month of data available. It was up 3% from a year earlier.

Excluding shelter, the CPI was up 2.7% on the year in September. The equivalent PriceStats measure for October showed annual inflation excluding housing costs was 2.7%-matching the highest rate since October 2023.

Government workers headed back to the office Thursday, but the scheduled October CPI report has not been forthcoming. It will take some time for the Bureau of Labor Statistics to catch up on the data releases it missed during the record-setting, 43-day shutdown.

The White House has suggested the October report on inflation is unlikely to be released due to the shutdown. Today, a White House official said jobs data for October is expected, but without the closely-followed unemployment rate.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Alstom hikes 2025 revenue outlook after first-half sales beat
data.description:
French train maker Alstom on Thursday lifted its 2025 organic sales outlook after posting half-year revenue above expectations, driven by strong demand for its high-speed trains.

Alstom, which makes trains and signalling systems for urban and regional rail networks, now anticipates organic sales will rise above 5%, compared with its previous guidance of 3% to 5%.

"Strong sales growth across all product lines demonstrates our ability to accelerate backlog delivery and achieve full-year growth above our initial expectations." said chief executive Henri Poupart-Lafarge.

Revenue rose 8% on a organic basis to 9.06 billion euros ($10.57 billion) in the first half of the year. Analysts had expected 8.97 billion euros, according to a company compiled consensus.

The increase was notably driven by a "strong performance" in Europe, where group revenue rose 8.5% year-on-year in the first half of the year.

Alstom has signed several major contracts this quarter - including a 6.9 billion zloty ($1.9 billion) contract with Poland's national long-distance rail operator PKP Intercity and a 1.4 billion euro Alstom high speed train order from French rail operator SNCF Voyageurs for its Eurostar routes.

The group confirmed the rest of its guidance for the year.

($1 = 0.8575 euros)

data.title: UBS takeover of Credit Suisse pushed up Swiss banks' funding costs, SNB says
data.description:
Swiss banks have to pay more currently to secure liquidity in the financial markets than two years ago, the Swiss National Bank said on Thursday, pointing to the 2023 collapse of Credit Suisse and takeover by rival UBS as one explanation.

Between mid-2023 and end-2024, banks' funding costs increased significantly for bonds and in the money market, SNB governing board member Petra Tschudin said at an event in Geneva.

These costs have recently declined somewhat, but they remain elevated, which can affect the pricing and granting of loans, she added.

One reason for the higher funding costs is the state-engineered merger of UBS and Credit Suisse in March 2023, which increased the number of customers turning to domestically focused banks, according to the SNB.

Customers who previously did business with both UBS and Credit Suisse looked for additional banking relationships to reduce their reliance on a single big bank, Tschudin said.

This has meant more customers switching to Swiss domestic banks, pushing up demand in the Swiss capital market, especially as local banks have less access to international borrowing.

The shift to smaller banks may have increased due to UBS not offering the same conditions as Credit Suisse, Tschudin said.

"Unlike the former Credit Suisse, many of the domestically focused banks have little or no presence abroad, so their investor base is narrower."

"This has led to a corresponding rise in domestic funding needs," she said, a situation which had pushed up funding costs as banks compete for liquidity.

Other factors included a rise in government bond yields as central banks bought less government debt and a more challenging liquidity environment in general.

"In summary, bank funding costs, as measured by swap spreads, have risen noticeably in recent quarters," Tschudin said.

She also said despite the rise in funding costs, credit growth in Switzerland remains robust.

"Our monetary policy thus remains effective," Tschudin said.

data.title: REG - Bankers Investment - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 5027H Bankers Investment Trust PLC 13 November 2025  

JANUS HENDERSON FUND MANAGEMENT UK LIMITED

THE BANKERS INVESTMENT TRUST PLC

LEGAL ENTITY IDENTIFIER: 213800B9YWXL3X1VMZ69

 

13 November 2025

THE BANKERS INVESTMENT TRUST PLC

("the Company")

Market purchase by the Company of its own shares

Notification is given that pursuant to the authority granted at the Annual General Meeting of the Company held on 25 February 2025 to make market purchases of the Company's own shares of 2.5p, a market purchase of 875,000 ordinary shares in the capital of the Company was made on 13 November 2025 at a price of 134.2263p per share to be held in treasury.

The issued share capital of the Company following this purchase will continue to be 1,315,102,830 ordinary shares of 2.5p each, of which 329,732,468   (25.1%) are held in treasury and have no voting rights.

For calculations of interests in the Company's voting rights, on a poll, members have one vote per share. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in The Bankers Investment Trust PLC is 985,370,362.

data.title: Winvest Group Ltd SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Winvest Group Ltd, a company specializing in content creation and advertising services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and market activities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $5,250 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $0 for the same period in 2024, representing an increase due to contracts with BioCalth International Inc and Roundtable of Chinese.
  • Gross (loss)/profit: $(2,062) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $0 for the same period in 2024, primarily due to higher costs incurred for projects.
  • Loss from operations: $(300,147) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(153,137) for the same period in 2024, reflecting increased operating expenses.
  • Net loss: $(305,445) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(358,081) for the same period in 2024, representing a decrease in net loss due to reduced other expenses.
  • Basic and diluted (loss) per common share: $(0.00) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, consistent with the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported an increase in revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to contracts with BioCalth International Inc and Roundtable of Chinese. This indicates a focus on expanding service offerings in content creation and advertising services.
  • Cost of Revenue: The cost of revenue increased for the three months ended September 30, 2025, due to new projects undertaken, highlighting the company's investment in expanding its service delivery capabilities.
  • Operating Expenses: There was a significant increase in operating expenses for the three months ended September 30, 2025, driven by advertising expenses, loss on issuance of promissory notes, and bad debt expenses. This reflects the company's efforts to enhance its market presence and manage financial instruments.
  • Other Expenses: The company experienced a decrease in other expenses for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to the absence of a loss on investment that was present in the previous year. This suggests improved management of investment activities.
  • Future Outlook: Management is actively pursuing additional equity financing and managing operating costs prudently. The company is also advancing film production projects to generate future revenue streams, indicating a strategic focus on diversifying revenue sources and improving liquidity.

SEC Filing: Winvest Group Ltd [ WNLV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: FDCTECH, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
FDCTECH, INC., a company specializing in investment and brokerage services, wealth management, and technology and software development, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational improvements, underscoring the company's strategic growth and enhanced efficiency.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $17.32 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous period's $18.18 million, indicating a decline of over 4.75% year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $9.45 million, showing an improvement from the previous year's $7.23 million, highlighting increased efficiency in cost management.
  • Operating Income: $0.90 million, compared to an operating loss of $1.69 million in the previous year, demonstrating a significant turnaround in operational performance.
  • Net Income: $0.44 million, a positive shift from a net loss of $0.86 million in the prior year, indicating improved profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.00, consistent with the previous period, reflecting the impact of a large number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in three primary business segments: Investment and Brokerage, Wealth Management, and Technology and Software Development. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, Investment and Brokerage generated $8,938,912, Wealth Management generated $4,976,601, and Technology and Software Development generated $3,400,210.
  • Geographical Performance: The company has expanded its global footprint through acquisitions, with key subsidiaries operating in Australia, Malta, the UK, and Cyprus. Alchemy Markets Ltd. (AML) in Malta and Alchemy Prime Ltd. (APL) in the UK are significant contributors to the company's brokerage services, with AML authorized to operate in multiple European countries.
  • New Product Launches: The company is developing the Condor Investing & Trading App, a simplified trading platform for stocks, ETFs, and other financial markets, expected to be commercialized by the end of the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Management plans to continue expanding its global customer base and enhancing its technological offerings. The company may seek external financing to support strategic growth initiatives, particularly in financial technologies.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company has demonstrated improved operational efficiency, with operating expenses decreasing as a percentage of total revenue over the past fiscal years.
  • Strategic Acquisitions: The company completed the acquisition of Alchemy Markets Ltd. and Alchemy Prime Ltd., enhancing its presence in the European market and expanding its service offerings in investment and brokerage.

SEC Filing: FDCTECH, INC. [ FDCT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: ELECTRO SENSORS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Electro Sensors Inc., a leading provider of industrial automation and OEM solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and market expansion efforts.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: Electro Sensors Inc. reported net sales of $2.748 million for Q3 2025, marking a 9.4% increase from Q3 2024. This growth was primarily driven by increased sales to industrial automation and OEM customers.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $1.458 million for Q3 2025, a 15.3% increase over Q3 2024. The gross margin improved to 53.1% from 50.4%, attributed to higher average selling prices.

Operating Income: Operating income for Q3 2025 was $0.181 million, a slight increase from $0.173 million in Q3 2024. This reflects higher sales offset by increased operating expenses.

Net Income: Net income for Q3 2025 was $0.208 million, a decrease from $0.238 million in Q3 2024. The decline was primarily due to higher operating expenses and lower interest income.

Net Income per Share: The net income per share for Q3 2025 was $0.06, compared to $0.07 for Q3 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The increase in net sales for the three and nine-month periods was primarily driven by increased sales to industrial automation and OEM customers.

Geographical Performance: Electro Sensors Inc. markets its products to customers located throughout the United States, Canada, Latin America, Europe, and Asia.

Future Outlook: The company continues to seek growth opportunities through its existing portfolio and potential acquisitions. It is also addressing supply chain challenges by seeking additional sources for components and modifying product designs to accommodate new components.

SEC Filing: ELECTRO SENSORS INC [ ELSE ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025
